generatedSentence: Ejected from his family, the maladjusted dropout sat by the seashore, a single earphone playing a vivid whistle, his mind a stretch of unreal histograms. He lucked into moments of softness, vamped up with feijoa tonics, ignoring the super jockeys on the outfield, often feeling cuffed and wrecked by the killjoys of life, his responses dulled. The noonday sun revealed omens: a brackish egg and a lurker's flashlight beam. An autopsy report, hinting at uranium and naphtha, jarred him. He had to backtrack; this wasn't nonsense. Raids on strangers’ household rockery, whispered rumors of lovesick nephews being forced to inject blanks, and a claustrophobic fear of fire, painted a grim impression. This wasn't just other people's taxes, it was war. With effrontery, he donned leather gauntlets and a pantaloon, a maker of his own destiny, rejecting the guide’s abject fear. Jingle and static filled his ear. The enemy's arctic penguin, a cuckoo among lapdogs, tried to unearth his resolve, but he slacked not. He knew this triumph would require a syringe, a mace, and a warrant to illustrate their judicial crimes and rubbishes their absurd ransom demands. He presented his origami to the newcomers, a welcome relief for the woozy, abject villagers. Though tickled by the giraffe's playful nards, his abdomen still ached with lumbago. The maker of this new world, free from the old maker's nonsense, now openly cultivated vegetables and drank ouzo from a teacup, a true family man. He had made a blanket, realizing his past trunk of sackcloth and dictionary nonsense was over.
panelSentences: Ejected from his family, the maladjusted dropout sat by the seashore, a single earphone playing a vivid whistle, his mind a stretch of unreal histograms. He lucked into moments of softness, vamped up with feijoa tonics, ignoring the super jockeys on the outfield, often feeling cuffed and wrecked by the killjoys of life, his responses dulled.

The noonday sun revealed omens: a brackish egg and a lurker's flashlight beam. An autopsy report, hinting at uranium and naphtha, jarred him. He had to backtrack; this wasn't nonsense. Raids on strangers’ household rockery, whispered rumors of lovesick nephews being forced to inject blanks, and a claustrophobic fear of fire, painted a grim impression. This wasn't just other people's taxes, it was war.

With effrontery, he donned leather gauntlets and a pantaloon, a maker of his own destiny, rejecting the guide’s abject fear. Jingle and static filled his ear. The enemy's arctic penguin, a cuckoo among lapdogs, tried to unearth his resolve, but he slacked not. He knew this triumph would require a syringe, a mace, and a warrant to illustrate their judicial crimes and rubbishes their absurd ransom demands.

He presented his origami to the newcomers, a welcome relief for the woozy, abject villagers. Though tickled by the giraffe's playful nards, his abdomen still ached with lumbago. The maker of this new world, free from the old maker's nonsense, now openly cultivated vegetables and drank ouzo from a teacup, a true family man. He had made a blanket, realizing his past trunk of sackcloth and dictionary nonsense was over.
rawPanelSentences: Ejected from his family, the maladjusted dropout sat by the seashore, a single earphone playing a vivid whistle, his mind a stretch of unreal histograms. He lucked into moments of softness, vamped up with feijoa tonics, ignoring the super jockeys on the outfield, often feeling cuffed and wrecked by the killjoys of life, his responses dulled.

The noonday sun revealed omens: a brackish egg and a lurker's flashlight beam. An autopsy report, hinting at uranium and naphtha, jarred him. He had to backtrack; this wasn't nonsense. Raids on strangers’ household rockery, whispered rumors of lovesick nephews being forced to inject blanks, and a claustrophobic fear of fire, painted a grim impression. This wasn't just other people's taxes, it was war.

With effrontery, he donned leather gauntlets and a pantaloon, a maker of his own destiny, rejecting the guide’s abject fear. Jingle and static filled his ear. The enemy's arctic penguin, a cuckoo among lapdogs, tried to unearth his resolve, but he slacked not. He knew this triumph would require a syringe, a mace, and a warrant to illustrate their judicial crimes and rubbishes their absurd ransom demands.

He presented his origami to the newcomers, a welcome relief for the woozy, abject villagers. Though tickled by the giraffe's playful nards, his abdomen still ached with lumbago. The maker of this new world, free from the old maker's nonsense, now openly cultivated vegetables and drank ouzo from a teacup, a true family man. He had made a blanket, realizing his past trunk of sackcloth and dictionary nonsense was over.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A protagonist with bright magenta cybernetic gauntlets and a teal-and-silver aviator jacket. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, a lone figure with an earphone, sits contemplatively on a futuristic seashore at dawn, long shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist, looking jarred, inspects a glowing schematic of a strange egg amidst shadows, medium close-up. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, now in a dynamic pose with mace and gauntlets, confronts a menacing robotic penguin, wide action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist, smiling and relaxed, shares ouzo from a teacup with villagers, showcasing vegetables in a peaceful, sunlit village, eye-level shot.

definitions.definition: To compel (a person or persons) to leave.
definitions.example: Andrew was ejected from his apartment for not paying the rent.

definitions.definition: To throw out or remove forcefully.

definitions.definition: A transducer that converts electric signals into sound and is held near the ear, especially as part of a telephone; an earpiece or headphone.

Anything shaped like an egg, such as an Easter egg or a chocolate egg.
A swelling on one's head, usually large or noticeable, associated with an injury.

A foolish or obnoxious person.

definitions.definition: A person who is regarded as having not yet realized they are transgender, has not yet come out, or is in the early stages of transitioning.

To throw eggs at.
To dip in or coat with beaten egg.

definitions.definition: A device designed to be placed in the mouth and blown, or driven by steam or some other mechanism, to make a whistling sound.

To make a shrill, high-pitched sound by forcing air through the mouth. To produce a whistling sound, restrictions to the flow of air are created using the teeth, tongue and lips.

definitions.definition: To move in such a way as to create a whistling sound.

definitions.definition: To send, signal, or call by a whistle.
Badly adjusted to the demands and stresses of daily living; unable to cope.
The coastal land bordering a sea or an ocean.
The foreshore, the strip of land between low water and high water.
Of excellent quality, superfine.
Better than average, better than usual; wonderful.
Very; extremely (used like the prefix super-).

definitions.definition: To patch, repair, or refurbish.

To cobble together, to extemporize, to improvise.
(shoemaking) To attach a vamp (to footwear).
To travel by foot; to walk.
To delay or stall for time, as for an audience.

definitions.definition: To seduce or exploit someone.
A felt-tipped permanent marker.
(of perception) Clear, detailed or powerful.
(of an image) Bright, intense or colourful.
Full of life, strikingly alive.

This is frequently accompanied by a message in the form of a hashtag that is posted in the broadcast's chat by the viewers.

The vivid perception of something as it is experienced, in contrast to ideas or thoughts drawn from memory or the imagination.
To manipulate a blank key within a lock so as to mark it with impressions of the shape of the lock, which facilitates creation of a duplicate key.
Something which portends or is perceived to portend either a good or evil event or circumstance in the future, or which causes a foreboding; a portent or augury.
definitions.example: a rise in imports might be an omen of economic recovery

definitions.definition: A thing of prophetic significance.
definitions.example: a sign of ill omen

definitions.definition: To be an omen of.
To divine or predict from omens.
The two-note sound made by the cuckoo.
A Barbadian food made from mashed okra and cornmeal.
Any of various birds, of the family Cuculidae, famous for laying its eggs in the nests of other species; but especially the common cuckoo, Cuculus canorus, that has a characteristic two-note call.
The sound of that particular bird.
The bird-shaped figure found in cuckoo clocks.
The cuckoo clock itself.
A person who inveigles themselves into a place where they should not be (used especially in the phrase a cuckoo in the nest).
Someone who is crazy.
To make the call of a cuckoo.
To repeat something incessantly.
Crazy; not sane.
To touch repeatedly or stroke delicately in a manner which causes laughter, pleasure and twitching.
definitions.example: He tickled Nancy's tummy, and she started to giggle.

definitions.definition: To unexpectedly touch or stroke delicately in a manner which causes displeasure or withdrawal.
definitions.example: A stranger tickled Nancy's tummy, causing her to scream in fear.

definitions.definition: (of a body part) To feel as if the body part in question is being tickled.
definitions.example: My nose tickles, and I'm going to sneeze!

definitions.definition: To appeal to someone's taste, curiosity etc.
To cause delight or amusement in.
definitions.example: He was tickled to receive such a wonderful gift.

definitions.definition: To feel titillation.
To catch fish in the hand (usually in rivers or smaller streams) by manually stimulating the fins.
To be excited or heartened.
Money paid to the government other than for transaction-specific goods and services.
A burdensome demand.
definitions.example: a heavy tax on time or health

definitions.definition: A task exacted from one who is under control; a contribution or service, the rendering of which is imposed upon a subject.
Charge; censure
A lesson to be learned.
To impose and collect a tax from (a person or company).
definitions.example: Some think to tax the wealthy is the fairest.

definitions.definition: To impose and collect a tax on (something).
definitions.example: Some think to tax wealth is destructive of a private sector.

definitions.definition: To make excessive demands on.
definitions.example: Do not tax my patience.

definitions.definition: To accuse.
To examine accounts in order to allow or disallow items.
A heavy, loosely woven fabric, usually large and woollen, used for warmth while sleeping or resting.
definitions.example: The baby was cold, so his mother put a blanket over him.

definitions.definition: A layer of anything.
definitions.example: The city woke under a thick blanket of fog.

definitions.definition: A thick rubber mat used in the offset printing process to transfer ink from the plate to the paper being printed.
definitions.example: A press operator must carefully wash the blanket whenever changing a plate.

definitions.definition: A streak or layer of blubber in whales.
To cover with, or as if with, a blanket.
definitions.example: A fresh layer of snow blanketed the area.

definitions.definition: To traverse or complete thoroughly.
definitions.example: The salesman blanketed the entire neighborhood.

definitions.definition: To toss in a blanket by way of punishment.
To take the wind out of the sails of (another vessel) by sailing to windward of it.
To nullify the impact of someone or something.
General; covering or encompassing everything.
definitions.example: They sought to create a blanket solution for all situations.

definitions.definition: A ruminant, of the genus Giraffa, of the African savannah with long legs and highly elongated neck, which make it the tallest living animal; yellow fur patterned with dark spots, often in the form of a network; and two or more short, skin-covered horns, so-called; strictly speaking the horn-like projections are ossicones.
A giraffe unicycle.
A laugh.
definitions.example: Are you having a giraffe?!

definitions.definition: Someone who guides, especially someone hired to show people around a place or an institution and offer information and explanation.
definitions.example: The guide led us around the museum and explained the exhibits.

definitions.definition: A document or book that offers information or instruction; guidebook.
A sign that guides people; guidepost.
Any marking or object that catches the eye to provide quick reference.
A device that guides part of a machine, or guides motion or action.
A spirit believed to speak through a medium.
A member of a group marching in formation who sets the pattern of movement or alignment for the rest.
An aging buffoon.
Trousers reminiscent of the tight-fitting leggings traditionally worn by a pantaloon.
A kind of fabric.

A small toy dog, kept as household pet, whose light weight and companionable temperament make it both suited and disposed to spend time resting in the comfort of its master's lap; a dog bred to behave in this manner.
(by extension) A person who behaves in a servile manner, such as a sycophantic employee or a fawning lover.
Collectively, all the persons who live in a given house; a family including attendants, servants etc.; a domestic or family establishment.
A line of ancestry; a race or house.
Belonging to the same house and family.
Found in or having its origin in a home.
Widely known to the public; familiar.
definitions.example: a household word; a household name

definitions.definition: A dealer in ribbons, buttons, thread, needles and similar sewing goods.
A men's outfitter.
A member of the Worshipful Company of Haberdashers, a livery company.
The Japanese art of paper folding.
A piece of art made by folding paper.
The materials science technology that applies the art of origami to products.
The materials science that studies origami applications in various material.
The mathematics field that studies folding 2-d surfaces into 3-d structures, using construct folds and creases akin to that from the art of origami.
An other, another (person, etc), more often rendered as another.
definitions.example: I'm afraid little Robbie does not always play well with others.

definitions.definition: The other one; the second of two.
definitions.example: One boat is not better than the other.

definitions.definition: To regard, label or treat as an "other", as not part of the same group; to view as different and alien.
To treat as different or separate; segregate; ostracise.
See other (determiner) below
Second.
definitions.example: I get paid every other week.

definitions.definition: Alien.
Different.
Left, as opposed to right.
Apart from; in the phrase "other than".
definitions.example: Other than that, I'm fine.

definitions.definition: Otherwise.
definitions.example: If you think other. — Shakespeare.

definitions.definition: A (usually self-sustaining) chemical reaction involving the bonding of oxygen with carbon or other fuel, with the production of heat and the presence of flame or smouldering.
An instance of this chemical reaction, especially when intentionally created and maintained in a specific location to a useful end (such as a campfire or a hearth fire).
definitions.example: We sat about the fire singing songs and telling tales.

definitions.definition: The occurrence, often accidental, of fire in a certain place, causing damage and danger.
definitions.example: During hot and dry summers many fires in forests are caused by regardlessly discarded cigarette butts.

definitions.definition: The aforementioned chemical reaction of burning, considered one of the Classical elements or basic elements of alchemy.
A heater or stove used in place of a real fire (such as an electric fire).
The elements necessary to start a fire.
definitions.example: The fire was laid and needed to be lit.

definitions.definition: The bullets or other projectiles fired from a gun.
definitions.example: The fire from the enemy guns kept us from attacking.

definitions.definition: Strength of passion, whether love or hate.
Liveliness of imagination or fancy; intellectual and moral enthusiasm.
Splendour; brilliancy; lustre; hence, a star.
A severe trial; anything inflaming or provoking.
Red coloration in a piece of opal.
A small French coin, originally of silver, afterwards of copper, worth 5 deniers; also a silver coin of Henry V current in the parts of France then held by the English, worth about 8 pence .
A nonplus .
The white spot in the centre of a target; hence the object to which anything is directed or aimed, the range of such aim .
A lot by which nothing is gained; a ticket in a lottery on which no prize is indicated .
An empty space; a void, for example on a paper .

(now chiefly U.S.) A document, paper, or form with spaces left blank to be filled up at the pleasure of the person to whom it is given (e.g. a blank charter, ballot, form, contract, etc.), or as the event may determine; a blank form .
Blank verse .
A piece of metal (such as a coin, screw, nuts), cut and shaped to the required size of the thing to be made, and ready for the finishing operations; (coining) the disc of metal before stamping .
A vacant space, place, or period; a void .
The 1 / 230400 of a grain .
An empty space in one's memory; a forgotten item or memory .
A dash written in place of an omitted letter or word
The space character; the character resulting from pressing the space-bar on a keyboard.
A domino without points on one or both of its divisions.
definitions.example: the double blank

definitions.definition: Short for blank cartridge. .
(in the expression ‘shooting blanks’) An ineffective effort which achieves nothing .
To make void; to erase.
definitions.example: I blanked out my previous entry.

definitions.definition: To ignore (a person) deliberately.
definitions.example: She blanked me for no reason.

definitions.definition: To prevent from scoring, for example in a sporting event.
definitions.example: England blanks Wales to advance to the final.

definitions.definition: To become blank.
To be temporarily unable to remember.
definitions.example: I'm blanking on her name right now.

definitions.definition: An instance of exhibiting, or something exhibited.
A large-scale public showing of objects or products.
definitions.example: There was an art exhibition on in the town hall.

definitions.definition: A financial award or prize given to a student (who becomes an exhibitioner) by a school or university, usually on the basis of academic merit.
A game which does not impact the standings for any major cup or competition.
The fat surrounding the belly.
The belly, or that part of the body between the thorax and the pelvis, not including the back; or in some lower vertebrates, the portion between the cardiac and caudal regions.

The cavity of the belly, which is lined by the peritoneum, and contains the viscera; often restricted in humans to the part between the diaphragm and the commencement of the pelvis, the remainder being called the pelvic cavity.
definitions.example: He was all bent over complaining of pains in the abdomen.

definitions.definition: The posterior section of the body, behind the thorax, in insects, crustaceans, and other Arthropoda.
The act of greeting someone’s arrival, especially by saying "Welcome!"; reception.
The utterance of such a greeting.
Kind reception of a guest or newcomer.
definitions.example: We entered the house and found a ready welcome.

definitions.definition: The state of being a welcome guest.
To affirm or greet the arrival of someone, especially by saying "Welcome!".
To accept something willingly or gladly.
definitions.example: We welcome suggestions for improvement.

definitions.definition: Whose arrival is a cause of joy; received with gladness; admitted willingly to the house, entertainment, or company.
definitions.example: Refugees welcome in London!

definitions.definition: Producing gladness.
definitions.example: a welcome present;  welcome news

definitions.definition: Free to have or enjoy gratuitously.
definitions.example: You are welcome to the use of my library.

definitions.definition: Greeting given upon someone's arrival.
Ellipsis of you're welcome.
A tough material produced from the skin of animals, by tanning or similar process, used e.g. for clothing.
A piece of the above used for polishing.
A cricket ball or football.
(plural: leathers) clothing made from the skin of animals, often worn by motorcycle riders.
A good defensive play
A punch.
The skin.
To cover with leather.
To strike forcefully.
definitions.example: He leathered the ball all the way down the street.

definitions.definition: To beat with a leather belt or strap.
Made of leather.
Referring to one who wears leather clothing (motorcycle jacket, chaps over 501 jeans, boots), especially as a sign of sadomasochistic homosexuality.
Someone who makes; a person or thing that makes or produces something.
(usually capitalized and preceded by the) God.
A poet.
Someone who signs a promissory note, thereby becoming responsible for payment.
A person whom one does not know; a person who is neither a friend nor an acquaintance.
definitions.example: That gentleman is a stranger to me.  Children are taught not to talk to strangers.

definitions.definition: An outsider or foreigner.
One not admitted to communion or fellowship.
A newcomer.
One who has not been seen for a long time.
definitions.example: Hello, stranger!

definitions.definition: One not belonging to the family or household; a guest; a visitor.
One not privy or party to an act, contract, or title; a mere intruder or intermeddler; one who interferes without right.
definitions.example: Actual possession of land gives a good title against a stranger having no title.

definitions.definition: A superstitious premonition of the coming of a visitor by a bit of stalk in a cup of tea, the guttering of a candle, etc.
A coarse hessian style of cloth used to make sacks.
(Usually paired with 'ashes'), garments worn as an act of penance. Now often used figuratively.
definitions.example: After he realised the gravity of his crime he spent some time wearing sackcloth and ashes.

definitions.definition: A reference work with a list of words from one or more languages, normally ordered alphabetically, explaining each word's meaning, and sometimes containing information on its etymology, pronunciation, usage, translations, and other data.
(preceded by the) A synchronic dictionary of a standardised language held to only contain words that are properly part of the language.
(by extension) Any work that has a list of material organized alphabetically; e.g., biographical dictionary, encyclopedic dictionary.
An associative array, a data structure where each value is referenced by a particular key, analogous to words and definitions in a physical dictionary.
To look up in a dictionary.
To add to a dictionary.
To compile a dictionary.
The sound of metal or glass clattering against itself.
definitions.example: He heard the jingle of her keys in the door and turned off the screen.

definitions.definition: A small piece of metal attached to a musical instrument, such as a tambourine, so as to make a jangling sound when the instrument is played.
definitions.example: Her tambourine didn't come with any jingles attached.

definitions.definition: A memorable short song, or in some cases a snippet of a popular song with its lyrics modified, used for the purposes of advertising a product or service in a TV or radio commercial.
definitions.example: That used-car dealership's jingle has been stuck in my head since we heard that song.

definitions.definition: A carriage drawn by horses.
A brief phone call; a ring.
definitions.example: Give me a jingle when you find out something.

definitions.definition: A jingle shell.
Coin money.
To make a noise of metal or glass clattering against itself.
definitions.example: The beads jingled as she walked.

definitions.definition: To cause to make a noise of metal or glass clattering against itself.
definitions.example: She jingled the beads as she walked.

definitions.definition: To rhyme or sound with a jingling effect.
That branch of government which is responsible for maintaining the courts of law and for the administration of justice.
Of or relating to the administration of justice.
Of or relating to the court system or the judicial branch of government.
Specified by a civil bill court under the terms of the Land Law (Ireland) Act, 1881
Of or relating to judgeship or the judiciary, the collective body of judges.
A small cup, usually with a handle, used for drinking tea; normally sits in a saucer as part of a tea set.
A unit of measure; a teacupful.
Of an animal, smaller than average.
A person in the lowest and most despicable condition; a castaway; outcast.
Sunk to or existing in a low condition, state, or position.
Cast down in spirit or hope; degraded; servile; grovelling; despicable; lacking courage; offered in a humble and often ingratiating spirit.
Showing utter hopelessness, helplessness; showing resignation; wretched.
Rejected; cast aside.
A passage in text that is about a different subject from the preceding text, marked by commencing on a new line, the first line sometimes being indented.
A mark or note set in the margin to call attention to something in the text, such as a change of subject.
An offset of 16 bytes in Intel memory architectures.
To sort text into paragraphs.
Backache of the lumbar region or lower back, which can be caused by muscle strain or a slipped disk.
A heavy fighting club.
definitions.example: 1786, The Mace is an ancient weapon, formerly much used by cavalry of all nations, and likewise by ecclesiastics, who in consequence of their tenures, frequently took the field, but were by a canon of the church forbidden to wield the sword. — Francis Grose, A Treatise on Ancient Armour and Weapons, page 51.

definitions.definition: A ceremonial form of this weapon.
definitions.example: 1598, I am a king that find thee; and I know 'Tis not the balm, the sceptre, and the ball, The sword, the mace, the crown imperial, The intertissued robe of gold and pearl... — William Shakespeare, Henvry V, Act IV, Scene I, line 259.

definitions.definition: A long baton used by some drum majors to keep time and lead a marching band. If this baton is referred to as a mace, by convention it has a ceremonial often decorative head, which, if of metal, usually is hollow and sometimes intricately worked.
An officer who carries a mace as an emblem of authority.
A knobbed mallet used by curriers in dressing leather to make it supple.
A billiard cue.
To hit someone or something with a mace.
A device used for injecting or drawing fluids through a membrane.
A device consisting of a hypodermic needle, a chamber for containing liquids, and a piston for applying pressure (to inject) or reducing pressure (to draw); a hypodermic syringe.
To clean, or inject fluid, by means of a syringe.
definitions.example: Have your ears syringed! They're so dirty!

definitions.definition: A son of one's sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either a son of one's brother (fraternal nephew) or a son of one's sister (sororal nephew).
A son of one's child.
Nardostachys jatamansi, a flowering plant of the valerian family that grows in the Himalayas, used as a perfume, an incense, a sedative, and an herbal medicine.
A fragrant oil from the plant, formerly much prized.
American spikenard (Aralia racemosa), a North American perennial herb with an aromatic root.
(1980s, usually plural) Testicles.
definitions.example: The soccer ball hit me right in the nards!

definitions.definition: Any plant.
A plant raised for some edible part of it, such as the leaves, roots, fruit or flowers, but excluding any plant considered to be a fruit, grain, herb, or spice in the culinary sense.
The edible part of such a plant.
A person whose brain (or, infrequently, body) has been damaged so that they cannot interact with the surrounding environment; a person in a persistent vegetative state.
To shed light upon.
To clarify something by giving, or serving as, an example or a comparison.
definitions.example: We illustrate our definitions by including quotations or simple examples.

definitions.definition: To provide a book or other publication with pictures, diagrams or other explanatory or decorative features.
definitions.example: The economics textbook was illustrated with many graphs.

definitions.definition: To give renown or honour to; to make illustrious.
An anise-flavoured aperitif, originating in Greece.
A serving of this drink.
A military cloak or long coat worn by soldiers or horsemen in the 16th and 17th centuries.
A coarse, loose cloak or gown, worn by women, sailors, shepherds, countryfolk etc.
An item of clerical clothing: a long, sheath-like, close-fitting, ankle-length robe worn by clergy members of some Christian denominations.
(heading, biological) Part of a body.
(heading) A container.
(heading) A channel for flow of some kind.
In software projects under source control: the most current source tree, from which the latest unstable builds (so-called "trunk builds") are compiled.
The main line or body of anything.
definitions.example: the trunk of a vein or of an artery, as distinct from the branches

definitions.definition: A large pipe forming the piston rod of a steam engine, of sufficient diameter to allow one end of the connecting rod to be attached to the crank, and the other end to pass within the pipe directly to the piston, thus making the engine more compact.
Shorts used for swimming (swim trunks).
To lop off; to curtail; to truncate.
To extract (ores) from the slimes in which they are contained, by means of a trunk.
(of water) Salty or slightly salty, as a mixture of fresh and sea water, such as that found in estuaries.
Distasteful; unpleasant; not appealing to the taste.
Repulsive
Someone who is hostile to, feels hatred towards, opposes the interests of, or intends injury to someone else.
definitions.example: Crush the enemy!

definitions.definition: A hostile force or nation; a fighting member of such a force or nation.
definitions.example: Rally together against a common enemy.

definitions.definition: Something harmful or threatening to another
To make an enemy of.
Of, relating to, or belonging to an enemy
Refuse, waste, garbage, junk, trash.
definitions.example: The rubbish is collected every Thursday in Gloucester, but on Wednesdays in Cheltenham.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An item, or items, of low quality.
definitions.example: Much of what they sell is rubbish.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Nonsense.
definitions.example: Everything the teacher said during that lesson was rubbish. How can she possibly think that a bass viol and a cello are the same thing?

definitions.definition: Debris or ruins of buildings.
To criticize, to denigrate, to denounce, to disparage.
Money paid for the freeing of a hostage.
definitions.example: They were held for two million dollars ransom.

definitions.definition: The release of a captive, or of captured property, by payment of a consideration.
definitions.example: prisoners hopeless of ransom

definitions.definition: A sum paid for the pardon of some great offence and the discharge of the offender; also, a fine paid in lieu of corporal punishment.
(14th century) To deliver, especially in context of sin or relevant penalties.
To pay a price to set someone free from captivity or punishment.
definitions.example: to ransom prisoners from an enemy

definitions.definition: To exact a ransom for, or a payment on.
definitions.example: Such lands as he had rule of he ransomed them so grievously, and would tax the men two or three times in a year. — Berners.

definitions.definition: To make real; to convert from the imaginary or fictitious into reality; to bring into real existence
definitions.example: The objectives of the project were never fully realized.

definitions.definition: To become aware of (a fact or situation, especially of something that has been true for a long time).
definitions.example: He realized that he had left his umbrella on the train.

definitions.definition: To cause to seem real; to sense vividly or strongly; to make one's own in thought or experience.
To acquire as an actual possession; to obtain as the result of plans and efforts; to gain; to get
definitions.example: to realize large profits from a speculation

definitions.definition: To convert any kind of property into money, especially property representing investments, such as shares, bonds, etc.
definitions.example: By realizing the company's assets, the liquidator was able to return most of the shareholders' investments.

definitions.definition: To convert into real property; to make real estate of.
To turn an abstract linguistic object into actual language, especially said of a phoneme's conversion into speech sound.
definitions.example: The southern /v/ is realized as the voiced approximant [ʋ].

definitions.definition: Authorization or certification; a sanction, as given by a superior.
Something that provides assurance or confirmation; a guarantee or proof.
definitions.example: a warrant of authenticity; a warrant for success

definitions.definition: An order that serves as authorization; especially a voucher authorizing payment or receipt of money.
An option, usually issued together with another security and with a term at issue greater than a year, to buy other securities of the issuer.
A judicial writ authorizing an officer to make a search, seizure, or arrest, or to execute a judgment.
definitions.example: an arrest warrant issued by the court

definitions.definition: Short for warrant officer.
A document certifying that a motor vehicle meets certain standards of mechanical soundness and safety; a warrant of fitness.
A defender, a protector.
Underclay in a coal mine.
To protect, keep safe (from danger).
To give (someone) an assurance or guarantee (of something); also, with a double object: to guarantee (someone something).
To guarantee (something) to be (of a specified quality, value, etc.).
To guarantee as being true; (colloquially) to believe strongly.
definitions.example: That tree is going to fall, I’ll warrant.

definitions.definition: To authorize; to give (someone) sanction or warrant (to do something).
definitions.example: I am warranted to search these premises fully.

definitions.definition: To justify; to give grounds for.
definitions.example: Circumstances arose that warranted the use of lethal force.

definitions.definition: Any of several flightless sea birds, of order Sphenisciformes, found in the Southern Hemisphere, marked by their usual upright stance, walking on short legs, and (generally) their stark black and white plumage.
An auk (sometimes especially a great auk), a bird of the Northern Hemisphere.
A nun (association through appearance, because of the black and white habit).
A type of catch where the palm of the hand is facing towards the leg with the arm stretched downward, resembling the flipper of a penguin.
A spiny bromeliad with egg-shaped fleshy fruit, Bromelia pinguin.
Letters or words, in writing or speech, that have no meaning or pattern or seem to have no meaning.
definitions.example: After my father had a stroke, every time he tried to talk, it sounded like nonsense.

definitions.definition: An untrue statement.
definitions.example: He says that I stole his computer, but that's just nonsense.

definitions.definition: That which is silly, illogical and lacks any meaning, reason or value; that which does not make sense.
Something foolish.
A type of poetry that contains strange or surreal ideas, as, for example, that written by Edward Lear.
A damaged DNA sequence whose products are not biologically active, that is, that does nothing.
To make nonsense of;
To attempt to dismiss as nonsense; to ignore or belittle the significance of something; to render unimportant or puny.
To joke around, to waste time
Resulting from the substitution of a nucleotide in a sense codon, causing it to become a stop codon (not coding for an amino-acid).
Nonsensical
An emphatic rejection of something one has just heard and does not believe or agree with.
One who has recently come to a community; a recent arrival.
A new participant in some activity; a neophyte.
